004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor.DataType.*;
022
023import java.io.*;
024import java.math.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
027
028/**
029 * Specialized input stream for parsing CBOR streams (RFC 8949).
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Reads CBOR-encoded binary data, extracting major types and additional information from
033 * initial bytes and decoding values according to RFC 8949.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
036 *    <li class='note'>This class is not intended for external use.
037 *    <li class='note'>Indefinite-length encoding (RFC 8949 §3.2) is supported: arrays/maps decode via the
038 *       BREAK-aware container loop, and chunked text/byte strings (heads <c>0x7F</c>/<c>0x5F</c>) are
039 *       reassembled from their definite-length pieces.
040 * </ul>
041 *
042 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
043 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/CborBasics">CBOR Basics</a>
044 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8949.html">RFC 8949</a>
045 * </ul>
046 */
047public class CborInputStream extends ParserInputStream {
048
049   private long length;
050   private int lastInitialByte;
051   private DataType lastDataType;
052   // Single-byte pushback for indefinite-length container loops that need to peek for BREAK
053   // without consuming the next data item.  -1 means no pushback present.
054   private int pushedBackByte = -1;
055
056   /**
057    * Constructor.
058    *
059    * @param pipe The parser input.
060    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
061    */
062   protected CborInputStream(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException {
063      super(pipe);
064   }
065
066   @Override /* InputStream */
067   public int read() throws IOException {
068      if (pushedBackByte != -1) {
069         int b = pushedBackByte;
070         pushedBackByte = -1;
071         return b;
072      }
073      return super.read();
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Peeks one byte and returns whether it is the CBOR BREAK marker (0xFF).  Non-BREAK bytes
078    * are pushed back so the next {@link #read()} sees them.
079    *
080    * <p>
081    * Used by definite/indefinite-length container loops to detect the end of an indefinite
082    * container without consuming the next data item.
083    *
084    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the next byte is BREAK; <jk>false</jk> otherwise (or at EOF).
085    * @throws IOException If the underlying stream fails.
086    */
087   boolean peekBreak() throws IOException {
088      int b = read();
089      if (b == -1)
090         return false;
091      if (b == 0xFF) {
092         lastDataType = BREAK;
093         length = 0;
094         return true;
095      }
096      pushedBackByte = b;
097      return false;
098   }
099
100   /**
101    * Reads the initial byte and returns it.
102    *
103    * @return The initial byte (0-255), or -1 on EOF.
104    * @throws IOException If read fails.
105    */
106   int readInitialByte() throws IOException {
107      int b = read();
108      if (b == -1)
109         throw ioex("Unexpected end of CBOR input");
110      return b & 0xFF;
111   }
112
113   /**
114    * Extracts the major type from an initial byte (high 3 bits).
115    *
116    * @param initialByte The initial byte.
117    * @return Major type 0-7.
118    */
119   static int getMajorType(int initialByte) {
120      return (initialByte >> 5) & 0x07;
121   }
122
123   /**
124    * Extracts the additional information from an initial byte (low 5 bits).
125    *
126    * @param initialByte The initial byte.
127    * @return Additional info 0-31.
128    */
129   static int getAdditionalInfo(int initialByte) {
130      return initialByte & 0x1F;
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Reads the argument based on additional info.
135    * For 0-23, returns additionalInfo; for 24-27, reads 1/2/4/8 bytes big-endian.
136    *
137    * @param additionalInfo The additional info from the initial byte.
138    * @return The argument value.
139    * @throws IOException If read fails.
140    */
141   long readArgument(int additionalInfo) throws IOException {
142      if (additionalInfo <= 23)
143         return additionalInfo;
144      if (additionalInfo == 24)
145         return readUInt1();
146      if (additionalInfo == 25)
147         return readUInt2();
148      if (additionalInfo == 26)
149         return readUInt4();
150      if (additionalInfo == 27)
151         return readUInt8();
152      if (additionalInfo == 31)
153         throw ioex("Indefinite-length CBOR encoding not supported");
154      throw ioex("Reserved additional info value: {0}", additionalInfo);
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Reads 1 unsigned byte.
159    */
160   @SuppressWarnings({
161      "java:S4144" // Identical to readInitialByte by design; each method has distinct semantic purpose
162   })
163   int readUInt1() throws IOException {
164      int b = read();
165      if (b == -1)
166         throw ioex("Unexpected end of CBOR input");
167      return b & 0xFF;
168   }
169
170   /**
171    * Reads 2 bytes big-endian as unsigned integer.
172    */
173   int readUInt2() throws IOException {
174      return (readUInt1() << 8) | readUInt1();
175   }
176
177   /**
178    * Reads 4 bytes big-endian as unsigned long.
179    */
180   long readUInt4() throws IOException {
181      long l = readUInt1();
182      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
183      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
184      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
185      return l;
186   }
187
188   /**
189    * Reads 8 bytes big-endian as unsigned long.
190    */
191   long readUInt8() throws IOException {
192      long l = readUInt1();
193      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
194      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
195      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
196      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
197      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
198      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
199      l = (l << 8) | readUInt1();
200      return l;
201   }
202
203   /**
204    * Reads the next CBOR data item's type and length/argument, returning the DataType.
205    * The argument is stored and can be retrieved via {@link #readLength()}.
206    *
207    * @return The DataType of the next item.
208    * @throws IOException If read fails.
209    */
210   @SuppressWarnings({
211      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for CBOR data type reading
212   })
213   DataType readDataType() throws IOException {
214      int ib = readInitialByte();
215      lastInitialByte = ib;
216      int majorType = getMajorType(ib);
217      int additionalInfo = getAdditionalInfo(ib);
218
219      DataType dt;
220      switch (majorType) {
221         case 0 -> dt = UINT;
222         case 1 -> dt = NINT;
223         case 2 -> dt = BINARY;
224         case 3 -> dt = STRING;
225         case 4 -> dt = ARRAY;
226         case 5 -> dt = MAP;
227         case 6 -> dt = TAG;
228         case 7 -> {
229            switch (additionalInfo) {
230               case 20 -> dt = BOOLEAN;
231               case 21 -> dt = BOOLEAN;
232               case 22 -> dt = NULL;
233               case 23 -> dt = UNDEFINED;
234               case 25, 26, 27 -> dt = FLOAT;
235               case 31 -> dt = BREAK;
236               default -> dt = SIMPLE;
237            }
238         }
239         default -> throw ioex("Invalid major type: {0}", majorType);
240      }
241
242      lastDataType = dt;
243      if (dt == FLOAT) {
244         if (additionalInfo == 25)
245            length = 2;
246         else if (additionalInfo == 26)
247            length = 4;
248         else
249            length = 8;
250      } else if ((dt == ARRAY || dt == MAP || dt == STRING || dt == BINARY) && additionalInfo == 31) {
251         // Indefinite-length container or string.  Sentinel: length = -1.  Containers are driven by
252         // the BREAK-aware loop (shouldContinueContainer / peekBreak); indefinite text/byte strings
253         // are reassembled from their chunks in readBinary (RFC 8949 §3.2.3).
254         length = -1;
255      } else if (dt != BOOLEAN && dt != NULL && dt != UNDEFINED && dt != BREAK)
256         length = readArgument(additionalInfo);
257      else
258         length = dt == BOOLEAN ? -1 : 0;
259
260      return dt;
261   }
262
263   /**
264    * Returns the length/argument for the last read data type.
265    * For strings/binary: byte count. For arrays: element count. For maps: pair count. For TAG: tag number.
266    */
267   long readLength() {
268      return length;
269   }
270
271   /**
272    * Reads a boolean (must have been identified as BOOLEAN by readDataType).
273    */
274   boolean readBoolean() {
275      return lastInitialByte == 0xF5;
276   }
277
278   /**
279    * Reads an unsigned integer value (UINT) as long.
280    * The value was already consumed by readDataType/readArgument.
281    */
282   long readUnsignedLong() {
283      return length;
284   }
285
286   /**
287    * Reads a signed integer. For UINT, returns the value. For NINT, returns -1 - value.
288    */
289   long readSignedLong() {
290      if (lastDataType == UINT)
291         return readUnsignedLong();
292      if (lastDataType == NINT)
293         return -1 - readUnsignedLong();
294      throw new IllegalStateException("Expected integer type, got " + lastDataType);
295   }
296
297   /**
298    * Reads the last integer (UINT/NINT) as a {@link BigInteger}, preserving the full unsigned
299    * 64-bit magnitude.
300    *
301    * <p>
302    * Used when the target Java type is {@link BigInteger} so that values beyond the signed
303    * {@code long} range (CBOR major type 0 values {@code >= 2^63}, and major type 1 values
304    * {@code < -2^63}) widen losslessly instead of surfacing as a sign-wrapped {@code long}.
305    *
306    * @return The integer value as a {@link BigInteger}.
307    */
308   BigInteger readBigInteger() {
309      var magnitude = new BigInteger(Long.toUnsignedString(length));
310      if (lastDataType == UINT)
311         return magnitude;
312      if (lastDataType == NINT)
313         return BigInteger.valueOf(-1).subtract(magnitude);
314      throw new IllegalStateException("Expected integer type, got " + lastDataType);
315   }
316
317   /**
318    * Reads an int (for UINT/NINT when value fits in int).
319    */
320   int readInt() {
321      return (int)readSignedLong();
322   }
323
324   /**
325    * Reads a long (for UINT/NINT).
326    */
327   long readLong() {
328      return readSignedLong();
329   }
330
331   /**
332    * Reads a float. Handles half (0xF9), single (0xFA), and double (0xFB) precision.
333    */
334   float readFloat() throws IOException {
335      if (lastDataType == FLOAT) {
336         if (lastInitialByte == 0xF9) {
337            int half = (readUInt1() << 8) | readUInt1();
338            return halfFloatToFloat(half);
339         }
340         if (lastInitialByte == 0xFA)
341            return Float.intBitsToFloat((int)readUInt4());
342         if (lastInitialByte == 0xFB)
343            return (float)Double.longBitsToDouble(readUInt8());
344      }
345      throw new IllegalStateException("Expected float type, got " + lastDataType);
346   }
347
348   /**
349    * Reads a double. Handles half, single, and double precision.
350    */
351   double readDouble() throws IOException {
352      if (lastDataType == FLOAT) {
353         if (lastInitialByte == 0xF9)
354            return halfFloatToFloat((readUInt1() << 8) | readUInt1());
355         if (lastInitialByte == 0xFA)
356            return Float.intBitsToFloat((int)readUInt4());
357         if (lastInitialByte == 0xFB)
358            return Double.longBitsToDouble(readUInt8());
359      }
360      throw new IllegalStateException("Expected float type, got " + lastDataType);
361   }
362
363   private static float halfFloatToFloat(int half) {
364      int exp = (half >> 10) & 0x1F;
365      int mant = half & 0x3FF;
366      int sign = (half & 0x8000) << 16;
367      if (exp == 0) {
368         // Zero (mant == 0) or subnormal (mant != 0).  A half subnormal is mant * 2^-24; the
369         // previous (mant << 13) form reinterpreted the bits as a float32 subnormal (mant * 2^-149),
370         // which is effectively zero.  Compute mant * 2^-24 directly (exact for mant <= 1023) and
371         // re-apply the sign bit.
372         var v = mant / 16777216f;  // 16777216 == 2^24
373         return Float.intBitsToFloat(Float.floatToRawIntBits(v) | sign);
374      }
375      if (exp == 31)
376         return mant == 0 ? Float.intBitsToFloat(sign | 0x7F800000)
377            : Float.NaN;
378      return Float.intBitsToFloat(sign | ((exp + 112) << 23) | (mant << 13));
379   }
380
381   /**
382    * Reads a UTF-8 text string.
383    */
384   String readString() throws IOException {
385      byte[] b = readBinary();
386      return new String(b, UTF8);
387   }
388
389   /**
390    * Reads a byte string (or text string's raw bytes).
391    *
392    * <p>
393    * Handles both definite-length strings and indefinite-length strings (RFC 8949 §3.2.3): when the
394    * head carried additional-info 31 ({@code length == -1}) the chunked definite-length pieces are
395    * reassembled until the BREAK marker.
396    */
397   byte[] readBinary() throws IOException {
398      if (length == -1)
399         return readIndefiniteString();
400      var b = new byte[(int)length];
401      var bytesRead = read(b);
402      if (bytesRead != b.length)
403         throw ioex("Expected to read {0} bytes but only read {1}", b.length, bytesRead);
404      return b;
405   }
406
407   /**
408    * Reassembles an indefinite-length text/byte string (RFC 8949 §3.2.3) from its definite-length
409    * chunks, terminated by the BREAK marker (0xFF).
410    *
411    * <p>
412    * Per §3.2.3 every chunk must be a definite-length string of the same major type as the
413    * enclosing indefinite string, and nested indefinite chunks are not permitted.  Malformed input
414    * surfaces as a clean {@link IOException} rather than a {@link NegativeArraySizeException}.
415    */
416   private byte[] readIndefiniteString() throws IOException {
417      var expectedMajorType = getMajorType(lastInitialByte);
418      var baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
419      while (true) {
420         int ib = read();
421         if (ib == -1)
422            throw ioex("Unexpected end of CBOR input in indefinite-length string");
423         ib &= 0xFF;
424         if (ib == 0xFF)  // BREAK
425            break;
426         var chunkMajorType = getMajorType(ib);
427         var chunkInfo = getAdditionalInfo(ib);
428         if (chunkMajorType != expectedMajorType)
429            throw ioex("Invalid chunk major type {0} in indefinite-length string (expected {1})", chunkMajorType, expectedMajorType);
430         if (chunkInfo == 31)
431            throw ioex("Nested indefinite-length string chunk not allowed");
432         var chunkLen = (int)readArgument(chunkInfo);
433         var chunk = new byte[chunkLen];
434         var bytesRead = read(chunk);
435         if (bytesRead != chunk.length)
436            throw ioex("Expected to read {0} bytes but only read {1}", chunk.length, bytesRead);
437         baos.write(chunk, 0, chunk.length);
438      }
439      return baos.toByteArray();
440   }
441}
